images.alt: Aerial view of the par-5 6th 'The Gambler' at King's North showing the island fairway during the 2024 renovation
images.caption: The Gambler (#6) from above — the iconic island fairway that defines King's North, captured during the September 2024 front-nine renovation. This MBN gallery page hosts the post-renovation aerial set of The Gambler.
images.source: MBN.com (Myrtle Beach National official affiliated guide)
images.attribution: Courtesy of Myrtle Beach National / MBN.com — aerial photography by MBN editorial team, captured 9-19-2024
images.rights_note: Hosted on MBN.com gallery (Founders Group International). NOT public-domain. Reach out to MBN/Founders for editorial/press use license. Image is the single most important hero shot for the app — post-renovation aerial of The Gambler island fairway.

images.alt: Aerial overview of King's North at Myrtle Beach National showing the course property from above
images.caption: Skyline Perspectives: King's North from above — a property-wide aerial set showcasing The Gambler's island fairways and the course's dramatic bunkering. Captured 3-26-25 (between renovation stages).
images.source: MBN.com (Myrtle Beach National official affiliated guide)
images.attribution: Courtesy of Myrtle Beach National / MBN.com — aerial photography by MBN editorial team, captured 3-26-2025
images.rights_note: MBN gallery — request license for editorial/press use. Photos document the course between the 2024 front-nine reopening and the 2025 back-nine renovation.

images.alt: Post-renovation aerial showing reimagined bunkering and green complexes at King's North
images.caption: King's North, Elevated — full post-renovation aerial set captured January 7, 2026, showing the reimagined bunker styling, improved turf lines, and restored scale across Arnold Palmer's original vision.
images.source: MBN.com (Myrtle Beach National official affiliated guide)
images.attribution: Courtesy of Myrtle Beach National / MBN.com — aerial photography captured 1-7-2026
images.rights_note: POST-RENOVATION (fully complete) imagery — most current set available. Best choice if you need imagery that reflects the new 2026 course state. Request license from MBN.

images.alt: Aerial photo gallery from King's North reopening day after front-nine renovation, October 3, 2024
images.caption: Aerial set captured on King's North reopening day (10-3-2024) after the front-nine renovation. Includes hole-by-hole front-nine aerials — The Gambler, the par-3 4th, the dogleg 3rd.
images.attribution: Courtesy of Myrtle Beach National / MBN.com — captured 10-3-2024
images.rights_note: Front-nine post-renovation; back nine in these shots is still pre-renovation. Use for #1-9 hero imagery. Request license from MBN.

images.alt: Pre-opening aerial view of the newly renovated back nine at King's North, October 2025
images.caption: Pre-opening aerial set over the newly renovated back nine — dramatic changes to the bunkers and green complexes (including the par-3 12th SC bunkering and the reconfigured 18th) are easy to see. Captured October 2, 2025.
images.attribution: Courtesy of Myrtle Beach National / MBN.com — captured 10-2-2025
images.rights_note: Post-renovation back-nine aerials — best source for the new SC-bunker complex at the 12th and the reconfigured Bull's-Eye 18th. Request license from MBN.

images.alt: Hole-by-hole photo gallery of the newly renovated back nine at King's North, including hole 12 and hole 18
images.caption: Hole-by-hole photo browse of the renovated back nine — including the new 'SC' greenside bunkering at the par-3 12th and the complete overhaul of the par-4 18th 'Bull's-Eye.' Jim Maggio photography.
images.attribution: Photos by Jim Maggio, courtesy of Play Golf Myrtle Beach
images.rights_note: Jim Maggio is the credited photographer; Play Golf Myrtle Beach (Myrtle Beach Area Golf Course Owners Association marketing entity) hosts. THIS is the post-renovation gallery for holes 10-18 — includes the only confirmed photos of the new SC bunker shape at #12 and the reconfigured 18th. Request license from Play Golf Myrtle Beach / Jim Maggio.

images.alt: Hole-by-hole aerial photo tour of King's North, all 18 holes
images.caption: Complete hole-by-hole aerial photo tour of King's North by Myrtle Beach Golf Trips. Pre-renovation (2022 staff photography) — includes the original SC bunkering at #12 and the 40-bunker 18th 'Bull's-Eye' as Palmer left it.
images.source: Myrtle Beach Golf Trips (Golf Trips Staff)
images.attribution: Photos by Myrtle Beach Golf Trips staff (Jim Maggio aerial photography)
images.rights_note: PRE-RENOVATION (May 2022) — use for historical reference only or for holes/elements unchanged by the renovation. NOT representative of the 2026 course state. Request license from Myrtle Beach Golf Trips.

images.alt: Aerial photograph of King's North 18th hole 'Bull's-Eye' with bunker spectacle on the finishing hole
images.caption: Jim Maggio's favorite aerial of the King's North 18th 'Bull's-Eye' — 'Maybe the favorite shot I've gotten in the last seven years.' Pre-renovation imagery of the 40-bunker finisher.
images.source: Play Golf Myrtle Beach — 'Five More of Our Favorite Aerial Shots'
images.attribution: Photo by Jim Maggio for Play Golf Myrtle Beach (2021)
images.rights_note: Article explicitly identifies this as Maggio's signature aerial of the 18th. PRE-renovation. Use only if you want to evoke the historical 40-bunker 'Bull's-Eye' look; for the new 18th use the October 2025 / January 2026 MBN gallery instead.

images.alt: Aerial tour photography of King's North 18th hole 'Bullseye' with 40 bunkers from tee to green
images.caption: 'The Hole Story: No. 18 at King's North' — aerial tour photo set of the par-4 18th 'Bullseye' (pre-renovation), the finishing hole that historically featured 40 bunkers from tee to green.
images.attribution: Aerial photography for Play Golf Myrtle Beach (2022)
images.rights_note: PRE-renovation — represents the historical 40-bunker 18th. The 2024-25 renovation completely reconfigured this hole, dropping bunker count to single digits.

images.alt: Official King's North course gallery, hole-by-hole photography
images.caption: Official King's North hole-by-hole course gallery, organized by Front 9 (1-9) and Back 9 (10-18). The most likely source for the canonical hole-by-hole hero shots — though the gallery does NOT specify renovation date.
images.source: Myrtle Beach National (official hosted tee-time site)
images.attribution: Courtesy of Myrtle Beach National (Founders Group International)
images.rights_note: Official gallery, but image renovation-date is unclear. Pair with MBN.com renovation galleries to verify current state. Request license from MBN / Founders Group International.

images.alt: Editorial photography for the official 'New King's North' marketing campaign
images.caption: Official 'New King's North' page — features post-renovation editorial photography including hero shots of the rebuilt greens, reshaped bunkers, and signature holes for the 2026 reopening campaign. The lead landing-page hero is a strong candidate for atmosphere/landscape imagery.
images.attribution: Myrtle Beach National official marketing photography
images.rights_note: Official site, post-renovation marketing photography. Best source for editorial/coastal-premium look. Request license from Myrtle Beach National.

images.alt: View from the island fairway looking down hole 6 'The Gambler' at King's North
images.caption: Player's-eye view from the island fairway on The Gambler (#6) — taken from inside the bet, looking down the hole. By Flickr user tewiespix (golfcoursepix). Taken December 2008 (PRE-renovation).
images.attribution: Photo by Flickr user 'tewiespix' (golfcoursepix), uploaded November 17, 2012; taken December 20, 2008
images.rights_note: Marked 'All rights reserved' on Flickr — direct usage not permitted without explicit permission from the photographer. Direct image URL provided is a 500px embed; original sizes up to 3072x2304 available via /sizes/o/. PRE-renovation. Listed for documentary value (the only verified direct image URL surfaced) — use only as a reference for the kind of player's-eye shot you might commission or license elsewhere.

images.alt: GolfPass editorial photography of King's North post-renovation, par-3 17th
images.caption: Tim Gavrich / GolfPass post-renovation editorial shot of the appealing set-piece par-3 17th at the new King's North, captured spring 2026. Article also includes a hero header shot of the property.
images.attribution: Photo by Tim Gavrich / GolfPass, April 2026
images.rights_note: GolfPass editorial. POST-renovation, professionally photographed. Request license from GolfPass/NBC Sports for editorial use.

images.alt: King's North printed scorecard / hole diagram PDF (Sept 2021)
images.caption: Official Myrtle Beach National King's North printed scorecard / hole-diagram brochure, September 2021. PRE-renovation but the only publicly hosted yardage diagram. Useful as a 'yardage book' visual asset in the PWA.
images.attribution: Myrtle Beach National official scorecard, September 2021
images.rights_note: Official scorecard PDF, hosted on the MBN WordPress uploads directory. PRE-renovation yardages (new card not yet on the public site as of search date). Verify against the 2026 scorecard before relying on yardages.

images.caption: Legacy King's North scorecard PDF scan available from NorthMyrtleBeachGolf.com (linked on course profile page as 'Download Kings North Scorecard, PDF Scan, 849kb'). Pre-renovation but useful as a 'yardage book' diagram asset.
images.rights_note: Scorecard PDF link is on the profile page — older scorecard. Use only as design reference / historical artifact; do not represent as the current scorecard.

images.alt: Official photography of the Myrtle Beach National clubhouse, shared by King's North, West Course and SouthCreek
images.caption: Official Myrtle Beach National page featuring clubhouse photography. Note: the clubhouse is shared across all three MBN courses (King's North, West Course, SouthCreek) — it is not exclusive to King's North. Features the Arnold Palmer statue and tribute display.
images.rights_note: Shared 54-hole clubhouse. As of the 2024-25 renovation cycle Founders Group International was planning a major clubhouse renovation with a Spring 2026 reopen — verify whether photography reflects new or old clubhouse before publishing. Request license from MBN.
